name: claude-code-via-ide
description: A Cursor / VS Code extension that wraps the Claude Code CLI in a rich, editor-native chat interface — think Claude Desktop, but integrated with Cursor.
author: Keldon Rush (appcloud9.com)

ccvi.wsl.enabled.description: Enable WSL integration for running Claude

ccvi.wsl.nodePath.description: Optional path to Node.js in the WSL distribution. Only needed if Claude was installed via npm. Recent Claude installs ship as a native executable and don't require Node.

ccvi.wsl.claudePath.description: Path to Claude executable in the WSL distribution

ccvi.thinking.show.description: Default for whether Claude's summarized thoughts are shown (the Thoughts toggle in the prompt pane). Per-session changes from the toggle take precedence.

ccvi.thinking.effort.description: Default thinking effort/depth (e.g. low, medium, high, xhigh, max). Empty inherits the model's default. Per-session changes from the Effort picker take precedence; available levels are per-model.

ccvi.permissions.yoloMode.description: Enable Yolo Mode to skip all permission checks. Use with caution as Claude can execute any command without asking.

ccvi.executable.path.description: Custom path to the Claude Code executable. Leave empty to use the default 'claude' command.

ccvi.environment.variables.description: Custom environment variables to pass to Claude Code. Example: {"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY": "sk-..."}. To fail faster when a turn is stuck on slow credential/connection resolution, you can set CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_RETRIES (default 10 — lower it, e.g. "2", to give up sooner) and/or API_TIMEOUT_MS (default 600000 — the per-request ceiling). These trade resilience on flaky networks for a faster failure, so leave them unset unless you want that.

ccvi.environment.disabled.description: When enabled, custom environment variables are not passed to Claude Code.

ccvi.terminal.useIntegrated.description: Use Cursor's integrated terminal for slash commands. Uncheck to use an external terminal.

ccvi.terminal.externalApp.description: External terminal application name (e.g., iTerm2, Windows Terminal, kitty).

ccvi.terminal.customTemplate.description: Custom terminal launch template. Use {{command}} as placeholder for the command to run.

ccvi.terminal.borderColor.description: Border color for the prompt in terminal mode.

ccvi.terminal.fontColor.description: Font color for the prompt in terminal mode.

ccvi.modes.planCommand.description: Command sent when the Plan mode pill is clicked. Leave empty to use the built-in default (`/modes plan ./doc`). Sent verbatim to Claude, so you can point it at any slash command or mode directive.

ccvi.modes.agentCommand.description: Command sent when the Agent mode pill is clicked. Leave empty to use the built-in default (`/modes agent`). Sent verbatim to Claude, so you can point it at any slash command or mode directive.

ccvi.firstRun.hasShown.description: Whether the first-run experience has been shown. Uncheck to see it again on next launch.

ccvi.plans.editors.description: Editor registry for the /plans toIDE verb. Each row is { key, command, directory }: 'key' is the handle you pass to toIDE, 'command' is the CLI binary launched (must be on PATH), 'directory' is where the plan is placed (leading ~ expands to home, leading / is absolute, otherwise relative to the project). The 'cursor' row's directory is the only out-of-repo destination.

command.description: CLI binary to launch (must be on PATH)

directory.description: Destination dir: ~ = home, / = absolute, else relative to project

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.test:downloader: npm run compile && mocha --ui tdd "out/test/downloader*.test.js" --reporter spec --timeout 360000
scripts.test:downloader:unit: npm run compile && mocha --ui tdd out/test/downloader.test.js --reporter spec
